hero_subtitle: A lightweight, open-source macOS menu bar app for managing displays and creating virtual monitors.
features_title: Everything you need, nothing you don't
features_desc: Simple display management that lives in your menu bar and stays out of the way.
feat_toggle_desc: Toggle monitors on and off without unplugging them. Handles edge cases like disabling the main display safely.
feat_virtual_desc: Create virtual monitors with custom resolutions and device presets — iPhone, iPad, Mac, TV, and more.
feat_main_desc: Change your primary monitor from the menu bar. Menu bar and dock reposition automatically.
feat_hidpi_desc: Create Retina virtual displays. HiDPI and standard modes properly grouped and labeled.
feat_sleep_desc: Automatically handles display state across sleep cycles. Your setup is restored without manual intervention.
feat_color_desc: Prevents the ColorSync deadlock that occurs with identical monitors on macOS Sequoia.
vd_desc: macOS limits resolution when no physical monitor is connected. SimpleDisplay creates virtual displays that the system treats as real screens — perfect for remote access, testing, and headless setups.
vd_remote_desc: Full resolution over VNC, Screen Sharing, Parsec, AnyDesk, or TeamViewer.
vd_headless_desc: Run Mac minis and Mac Studios with no monitor attached at any resolution.
vd_responsive_desc: Test layouts at specific device resolutions without extra hardware.
vd_hidden_desc: Keep apps running on a virtual display while working on your main screen.
how_desc: SimpleDisplay uses macOS private frameworks to give you control that the built-in settings don't offer.
how_virtual_desc: Uses Apple's private CGVirtualDisplay API to create displays that appear as real monitors to macOS — useful for screen sharing specific resolutions, testing responsive layouts, or keeping apps running on a "hidden" screen.
how_disable_desc: Mirrors it to another active display via CGConfigureDisplayMirrorOfDisplay, effectively turning it off. This is reversed by removing the mirror relationship.
how_why_desc: These capabilities aren't available through public frameworks. SimpleDisplay can't be distributed on the Mac App Store, but it can do things no App Store app can.
start_desc: Download the DMG or build from source — your choice.
start_download_desc: Grab the latest DMG from GitHub Releases. Open it, drag to Applications, and you're done.
start_first_launch_desc: macOS will block the app since it isn't notarized. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security and click Open Anyway. This only needs to be done once.
start_build_desc: Clone the repo and build with a single command. Requires Xcode Command Line Tools.
lim_desc: SimpleDisplay uses private Apple APIs. Here's what that means in practice.
lim_1: Cannot be distributed on the Mac App Store — private APIs are not allowed by Apple's review guidelines.
lim_2: Virtual display refresh rate is capped at 60 Hz (API limitation).
lim_3: Display identification uses names, so two identical monitors may not be distinguishable in all scenarios.
lim_4: The CGVirtualDisplay API may change or be removed in future macOS versions.
community_desc: SimpleDisplay is free software. Built with insights from the macOS display management community, including BetterDisplay, DeskPad, and displayplacer. Contributions of all kinds are welcome.
footer: © 2026 SimpleDisplay · Licensed under GPL-3.0 · Made for macOS
